name: Kakaako
desc: Honolulu's urban renewal zone — new high-rise condos, murals, SALT complex, farmers market, Kewalo Basin energy.
insider: POW! WOW! Hawaii murals are scattered across warehouses and walls — download the mural map for a self-guided walking tour
SALT at Our Kaka'ako has the best curated food hall vibe — Highway Inn for Hawaiian food and Moku Kitchen for brunch
Saturday farmers market at Ward Village is smaller but more local than KCC — great produce and live music
Kewalo Basin boat harbor has affordable sunset sails and fishing charters that most visitors never discover
New condo towers at Ward Village start around $700K for a 1BR — this is one of the priciest new-build zones in Honolulu
Waterfront Park at Kakaako Gateway has a great walking path along the ocean — bring a skateboard, the concrete is smooth
Homeless encampments along the makai side of Kakaako have been an ongoing issue — certain blocks are rougher after dark
Honolulu Beerworks taproom on Cooke Street is one of the best craft brewery experiences on the island
Construction noise is constant on weekdays — new towers are going up everywhere through the late 2020s
The neighborhood transforms on weekends — dead quiet weekday nights but buzzing Saturday mornings at the markets
